Meta / CLI

Meta CLI

Core metadata command-line tools.

Command-line tools

Select an item from the navigation to open its reference page.

CLISummary
meta Meta workspace CLI.
meta-cli
meta-docs Author, inspect, merge, validate, and render modeled documentation workspaces.
meta-mesh MetaMesh makes repository workflows explicit: it names the workspaces involved, orders the executable steps, validates the inputs, and runs the operation from a model that engineers and agents can inspect.
meta-weave

meta

Commands

45 commands exposed by meta.

CommandSummary
meta bulk-insert Insert many instances from tsv/csv input.
meta check Check model and instance integrity.
meta delete Delete one instance.
meta deploy sqlserver Deploy SQL Server scripts.
meta export csv Export instances as CSV.
meta generate csharp Generate C# artifacts.
meta generate sql Generate SQL artifacts.
meta generate ssdt Generate SSDT artifacts.
meta graph inbound Show inbound relationships for an entity.
meta graph stats Show graph statistics.
meta help Show help.
meta import csv Import CSV data into a workspace.
meta import sql Import SQL metadata into a new workspace.
meta init Initialize a metadata workspace.
meta insert Insert one instance.
meta instance diff Diff instance data for two workspaces with byte-identical model.xml.
meta instance diff-aligned Diff mapped instance data using an explicit alignment workspace.
meta instance merge Apply an equal-model instance diff artifact to a target workspace.
meta instance merge-aligned Apply an aligned instance diff artifact to a target workspace.
meta instance relationship list List instance relationships.
meta instance relationship set Set an instance relationship.
meta instance rename-id Rename one instance id.
meta instance update Update one instance.
meta list entities List entities in the model.
meta list properties List properties for an entity.
meta list relationships List relationships for an entity.
meta model add-entity Add an entity.
meta model add-property Add a property to an entity.
meta model add-relationship Add a relationship.
meta model drop-entity Drop an entity.
meta model drop-property Drop a property.
meta model drop-relationship Drop a relationship.
meta model refactor property-to-relationship Convert a property into a relationship.
meta model refactor relationship-to-property Convert a relationship into a property.
meta model rename-entity Rename an entity.
meta model rename-model Rename the model.
meta model rename-property Rename a property.
meta model rename-relationship Rename a relationship role.
meta model set-property-required Change whether a property is required.
meta model suggest Suggest model improvements.
meta query Search instances with equals/contains filters.
meta status Show workspace summary and model/instance sizes.
meta view entity View one entity definition.
meta view instance View one instance row.
meta workspace merge Merge two full workspaces into a new workspace.

CommandSummary

Meta / CLI

meta-docs

Author, inspect, merge, validate, and render modeled documentation workspaces.

Commands

16 commands exposed by meta-docs.

CommandSummary
meta-docs author-page Add or update an authored documentation page in a MetaDocs workspace.
meta-docs contents List the documentation contents as a navigable tree.
meta-docs help Show help.
meta-docs import-cli Import an authored MetaCli workspace into a MetaDocs workspace.
meta-docs import-workspace-instances Import selected workspace instance documentation from a modeled opt-in policy.
meta-docs import-workspace-model Import generic model documentation from a Meta workspace.
meta-docs include-instance-entity Add or update an opt-in entity policy for workspace instance documentation.
meta-docs include-instance-property Add or update an opt-in property policy for workspace instance documentation.
meta-docs include-instance-relationship Add or update an opt-in relationship policy for workspace instance documentation.
meta-docs merge Merge multiple MetaDocs workspaces into a suite workspace.
meta-docs read Read one documentation subject.
meta-docs render-site Render a MetaDocs workspace as a static documentation site.
meta-docs search Search documentation subjects, descriptions, and details.
meta-docs show Show workspace contents and useful next commands.
meta-docs update-description Add or update an authored description for an existing documentation subject.
meta-docs validate Validate a MetaDocs workspace lifecycle and reference integrity.
meta-docs author-page Add or update an authored documentation page in a MetaDocs workspace. 12 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-docs author-page --body <value> --id <value> [--kind <value>] [--new-workspace <path>] [--parent <value>] [--path <value>] [--slot <value>] [--source-id <value>] [--source-name <value>] --summary <value> --title <value> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--body <value> Authored narrative body. <value> False
--id <value> Stable documentation subject id. <value> False
--kind <value> Optional subject kind. Default: Guide. <value> False
--new-workspace <path> New MetaDocs workspace to create. <path> False
--parent <value> Optional parent subject id. <value> False
--path <value> Optional display path. Default: title. <value> False
--slot <value> Optional narrative slot. Default: Summary. <value> False
--source-id <value> Optional authored documentation source id. <value> False
--source-name <value> Optional authored documentation source display name. <value> False
--summary <value> Short subject summary. <value> False
--title <value> Page title and subject display name. <value> False
--workspace <path> Existing MetaDocs workspace to update in place. <path> False
meta-docs contents List the documentation contents as a navigable tree. 3 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-docs contents [--depth <value>] [--view <value>] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--depth <value> Maximum tree depth. Default: 4. <value> False
--view <value> Optional documentation view id, name, or title. <value> False
--workspace <path> MetaDocs workspace to list. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False

--source-id <value> Optional stable documentation source id. <value> False

Meta / CLI

meta-mesh

MetaMesh makes repository workflows explicit: it names the workspaces involved, orders the executable steps, validates the inputs, and runs the operation from a model that engineers and agents can inspect.

Commands

11 commands exposed by meta-mesh.

CommandSummary
meta-mesh add-operation Add one declared operation.
meta-mesh add-step Add one executable step to an operation.
meta-mesh add-workspace Add one declared workspace.
meta-mesh help Show help.
meta-mesh new-workspace Create an empty MetaMesh workspace.
meta-mesh operations List declared operations.
meta-mesh run Run the steps declared for one operation.
meta-mesh show Show a compact mesh overview.
meta-mesh steps List steps for one operation.
meta-mesh validate Validate one operation before running it.
meta-mesh workspaces List declared workspaces.
meta-mesh add-operation Add one declared operation. 3 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-mesh add-operation [--description <value>] --name <value> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--description <value> Operation description. <value> False
--name <value> Operation name. <value> False
--workspace <path> MetaMesh workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
meta-mesh add-step Add one executable step to an operation. 10 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-mesh add-step [--arguments <arguments>] [--arguments-stdin] [--description <value>] --executable <path> [--expected-exit-code <value>] --name <value> --operation <value> [--previous-step <value>] [--working-directory <path>] [--workspace <path>]

Guidance

Step arguments support {workspace:<name>.path}, which expands to a declared workspace path, and {env:NAME}, which verifies NAME is non-empty and passes the variable name to the child process.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--arguments <arguments> Arguments passed to the executable. <arguments> False
--arguments-stdin Read executable arguments from standard input. False
--description <value> Step description. <value> False
--executable <path> Executable or script command to run. <path> False
--expected-exit-code <value> Exit code that marks the step as successful. Defaults to 0. <value> False
--name <value> Step name. <value> False
--operation <value> Operation name. <value> False
--previous-step <value> Previous step by step name. <value> False
--working-directory <path> Working directory, absolute or relative to the mesh root. <path> False
--workspace <path> MetaMesh workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
meta-mesh add-workspace Add one declared workspace. 5 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-mesh add-workspace [--description <value>] [--model <value>] --name <value> --path <path> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--description <value> Workspace description. <value> False
--model <value> Model name hosted by the workspace. <value> False
--name <value> Declared workspace name. <value> False
--path <path> Workspace path, absolute or relative to the mesh root. <path> False
--workspace <path> MetaMesh workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
meta-mesh help Show help. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-mesh help [--workspace <path>]

Options

--workspace <path> MetaMesh workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired

--name <value> Mesh name. <value> False
--root <path> Root path used to resolve relative workspace paths. <path> False

meta-mesh operations List declared operations. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-mesh operations [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> MetaMesh workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
meta-mesh run Run the steps declared for one operation. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-mesh run --operation <value> [--workspace <path>]

Guidance

Run performs validation before starting: ordered step chain, declared workspace paths, executable lookup, working-directory access, and non-empty {env:...} variables must all resolve first.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--operation <value> Operation name. <value> False
--workspace <path> MetaMesh workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
meta-mesh show Show a compact mesh overview. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-mesh show [--verbose] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--verbose Show workspace paths and step commands. False
--workspace <path> MetaMesh workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
meta-mesh steps List steps for one operation. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-mesh steps --operation <value> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--operation <value> Operation name. <value> False
--workspace <path> MetaMesh workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
meta-mesh validate Validate one operation before running it. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-mesh validate --operation <value> [--workspace <path>]

Guidance

Validate performs the same preflight as run but starts no child processes.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--operation <value> Operation name. <value> False
--workspace <path> MetaMesh workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
meta-mesh workspaces List declared workspaces. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-mesh workspaces [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> MetaMesh workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False

meta-convert sql-to-transform-script Convert MetaSql SQL modules to MetaTransformScript workspace. 6 options / 0 examples

Usage

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--allow-empty Create an empty MetaTransformScript workspace when selected module kinds have no convertible modules. False
--include-functions Convert function modules. If no include switch is provided, all module kinds are selected. False
--include-stored-procedures Convert stored procedure modules. If no include switch is provided, all module kinds are selected. False
--include-views Convert view modules. If no include switch is provided, all module kinds are selected. False
--out <path> Output MetaTransformScript workspace path. <path> False
--workspace <path> Source MetaSql workspace path. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
meta-convert transform-script-to-sql Convert MetaTransformScript SQL modules to MetaSql workspace. 3 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-convert transform-script-to-sql --database-name <value> --out <path> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--database-name <value> Target MetaSql database name. <value> False
--out <path> Output MetaSql workspace path. <path> False
--workspace <path> Source MetaTransformScript workspace path. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False

CommandSummary
meta-data-quality from-transform-workspace Create generated DQ views from a full MetaTransformScript workspace.
meta-data-quality help Show help.
meta-data-quality inspect Review the generated DQ pack and optional adjustments.
meta-data-quality promote Promote generated DQ candidates for SQL output.
meta-data-quality from-transform-workspace Create generated DQ views from a full MetaTransformScript workspace. 3 options / 0 examples

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired

meta-data-quality inspect Review the generated DQ pack and optional adjustments. 4 options / 0 examples

Usage

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--show-candidate-ids Include candidate ids in adjustment output. Implies --show-cases. False
--show-cases Show candidate adjustment cases. False
--top-cases <n> Show up to n candidate cases. Implies --show-cases. <n> False
--workspace <path> MetaDataQuality workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
meta-data-quality promote Promote generated DQ candidates for SQL output. 4 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-data-quality promote [--all] [--candidate-id <value>] [--candidate-kind <value>] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--all Promote every generated data-quality candidate. False
--candidate-id <value> Promote one generated candidate. May be provided more than once. <value> False
--candidate-kind <value> Promote generated candidates of one candidate family. May be provided more than once. <value> False
--workspace <path> MetaDataQuality workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False

meta-orchestration

Commands

12 commands exposed by meta-orchestration.

CommandSummary
meta-orchestration add-dependency Record an explicit success or failure dependency between tasks.
meta-orchestration add-order Record an explicit success dependency/order resolution in an orchestration workspace.
meta-orchestration allow-concurrent-append Allow concurrent execution for multiple Append effects on one object.
meta-orchestration execute Execute the current run plan by coordinating meta-pipeline worker processes.
meta-orchestration explain-issue Explain one analyzer issue and its participating pipelines.
meta-orchestration help Show help.
meta-orchestration infer Infer a MetaOrchestration workspace from a modeled MetaPipeline workspace.
meta-orchestration inspect Inspect a MetaOrchestration workspace.
meta-orchestration inspect-run-plan Inspect the planned task dependency graph.
meta-orchestration list-issues List analyzer issues recorded in an orchestration workspace.
meta-orchestration refresh-run-plan Refresh lock-aware run-plan rows in an orchestration workspace.
meta-orchestration set-lock-policy Record scoped lock compatibility for one object/effect interaction.
meta-orchestration add-dependency Record an explicit success or failure dependency between tasks. 6 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-orchestration add-dependency --condition success|failure --from-task <value> [--object <value>] [--reason <value>] --to-task <value> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--condition success|failure Whether the successor follows predecessor success or failure. success|failure: failure, success False
--from-task <value> Predecessor task selector. <value> False
--object <value> Object selector for object-scoped dependency resolution. <value> False
--reason <value> Reason recorded with the policy row. <value> False
--to-task <value> Successor task selector. <value> False
--workspace <path> MetaOrchestration workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--condition success|failure Dependency condition. Default: success. success|failure: failure, success False
--from-task <value> Predecessor task selector. <value> False
--object <value> Object selector for object-scoped dependency resolution. <value> False
--reason <value> Reason recorded with the policy row. <value> False
--to-task <value> Successor task selector. <value> False

meta-orchestration execute Execute the current run plan by coordinating meta-pipeline worker processes. 9 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-orchestration execute [--data-type-conversion-workspace <path>] [--max-degree-of-parallelism <n>] [--pipeline-db-connection-env <value>] --pipeline-workspace <path> [--run-artifacts-root <path>] [--worker-activation-timeout-seconds <n>] [--worker-control-pipe-connect-timeout-seconds <n>] [--worker-event-timeout-seconds <n>] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--data-type-conversion-workspace <path> Conversion policy workspace passed to child workers. <path> False
--max-degree-of-parallelism <n> Maximum concurrently granted pipeline tasks. Default: 1. <n> False
--pipeline-db-connection-env <value> Operational DB connection environment variable passed to child workers. <value> False
--pipeline-workspace <path> MetaPipeline workspace used by child pipeline workers. <path> False
--run-artifacts-root <path> Operational root for run journals, worker logs, and workspace execution leases. <path> False
--worker-activation-timeout-seconds <n> Startup/activation timeout. Omitted follows --worker-event-timeout-seconds; 0 disables activation timeout. <n> False
--worker-control-pipe-connect-timeout-seconds <n> Timeout while waiting for child workers to connect to the named pipe. 0 or omitted means no timeout. <n> False
--worker-event-timeout-seconds <n> Timeout for silent worker protocol periods. 0 or omitted means no timeout. <n> False
--workspace <path> MetaOrchestration workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--issue <value> Issue id or unique issue code. <value> False
--workspace <path> MetaOrchestration workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False

Usage

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--behavior serialize|allow Lock behavior for the object/effect pair. serialize|allow: allow, serialize False
--left-effect <value> Left write effect, such as Append, Replace, Mutation, KeyedUpsert, or ConditionalKeyedUpsert. <value> False
--object <value> Data object whose effect interaction is being resolved. <value> False
--reason <value> Reason recorded with the policy row. <value> False
--right-effect <value> Right write effect, such as Append, Replace, Mutation, KeyedUpsert, or ConditionalKeyedUpsert. <value> False
--workspace <path> MetaOrchestration workspace. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
Meta-BI / CLI

meta-pipeline

Commands

12 commands exposed by meta-pipeline.

CommandSummary
meta-pipeline add-executable-step Append one executable-backed task instance to the pipeline's serial task chain.
meta-pipeline add-pipeline Add one Pipeline instance to a MetaPipeline workspace.
meta-pipeline add-step Append one transform-backed task instance to the pipeline's serial task chain.
meta-pipeline create-pipeline-db Create or update the SQL Server MetaPipeline operational DB for diagnostic logs, audit logs, metrics, and failures; it does not store model truth.
meta-pipeline execute Execute a modeled pipeline's serial task chain with compact live progress including B/KB/MB/GB rate.
meta-pipeline execute-sqlserver Execute one SQL Server transform script with script selection options and compact live progress including B/KB/MB/GB rate.
meta-pipeline execute-step Execute exactly one modeled pipeline task by task name or id; diagnostic surface, while orchestration uses execute-worker.
meta-pipeline execute-worker Execute a modeled pipeline as an orchestration worker boundary over a named pipe; stdout and stderr are diagnostics only; preserves that pipeline context and handles GrantTask and StopPipeline.
meta-pipeline help Show help.
meta-pipeline inspect Show a compact MetaPipeline workspace summary.
meta-pipeline new-workspace Create an empty sanctioned MetaPipeline workspace.
meta-pipeline prune-pipeline-db Prune old completed RunDiagnosticsLog rows while preserving RunLog and audit lineage.
meta-pipeline add-executable-step Append one executable-backed task instance to the pipeline's serial task chain. 8 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-pipeline add-executable-step [--arguments <text>] --executable <path> --pipeline <value> [--step-name <value>] [--success-exit-code <n>] [--timeout-seconds <n>] [--working-directory <path>] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--arguments <text> Raw command-line arguments passed to the executable. <text> False
--executable <path> Executable path or executable name resolvable by the operating system. <path> False
--pipeline <value> Pipeline that receives the new serial step. <value> False
--step-name <value> Step name; omitted derives a deterministic name from the executable file name. <value> False
--success-exit-code <n> Expected process exit code. Default: 0. <n> False
--timeout-seconds <n> Process timeout seconds. 0 or omitted means no timeout. <n> False
--working-directory <path> Process working directory. <path> False
--workspace <path> Existing MetaPipeline workspace to update. Defaults to the current working directory. <path> False
meta-pipeline add-pipeline Add one Pipeline instance to a MetaPipeline workspace. 3 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-pipeline add-pipeline [--description <value>] --name <value> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--description <value> Pipeline description. <value> False
--name <value> Pipeline name. <value> False
--workspace <path> Existing MetaPipeline workspace to update. Defaults to the current working directory. <path> False
meta-pipeline add-step Append one transform-backed task instance to the pipeline's serial task chain. 14 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-pipeline add-step [--batch-size <n>] [--binding <value>] --binding-workspace <path> --execution-connection-env <value> --pipeline <value> --script <value> [--step-name <value>] [--target <sql-identifier>] [--target-connection-env <value>] [--target-data-type-system <value>] [--target-write <value>] [--timeout-seconds <n>] --transform-workspace <path> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--batch-size <n> Bounded in-memory row buffer size for InsertRows. Default: 1000. <n> False
--binding <value> Binding id when the selected script has multiple bindings. <value> False
--binding-workspace <path> MetaTransformBinding workspace used for binding selection. <path> False
--execution-connection-env <value> Environment variable named by the modeled execution connection. <value> False
--pipeline <value> Pipeline that receives the new serial step. <value> False
--script <value> TransformScript.Name or TransformScript.Id to model. <value> False
--step-name <value> Step name; omitted derives a deterministic name from the script name. <value> False
--target <sql-identifier> Target table identifier when a SELECT binding has multiple targets. <sql-identifier> False
--target-connection-env <value> Environment variable named by the modeled target connection. <value> False
--target-data-type-system <value> InsertRows target type family. Default: SqlServer. <value> False
--target-write <value> SELECT-kind target write model. The only supported value is insert-rows. <value> False
--timeout-seconds <n> SQL command and bulk-copy timeout seconds. 0 or omitted means no timeout. <n> False
--transform-workspace <path> MetaTransformScript workspace used for script selection. <path> False
--workspace <path> Existing MetaPipeline workspace to update. Defaults to the current working directory. <path> False

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--connection-env <value> Environment variable containing the SQL Server connection string. <value> False
--manifest-workspace <path> Deploy manifest workspace created by deploy-plan. <path> False
--source-workspace <path> Source MetaSql workspace used to create the manifest. <path> False
meta-sql deploy-plan Create a deploy manifest from a source MetaSql workspace and a live SQL Server database. 7 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-sql deploy-plan [--approval-file <path>] [--approve-drop-column <value>] [--approve-drop-table <value>] [--approve-truncate-column <value>] --connection-env <value> --out <path> --source-workspace <path>

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--approval-file <path> JSON file containing destructive approvals. <path> False
--approve-drop-column <value> Approve one exact destructive column drop in the form schema.table.column. <value> False
--approve-drop-table <value> Approve one exact destructive table drop in the form schema.table. <value> False
--approve-truncate-column <value> Approve one exact destructive column truncation in the form schema.table.column. <value> False
--connection-env <value> Environment variable containing the SQL Server connection string. <value> False
--out <path> Directory where the deploy manifest workspace will be written. <path> False
--source-workspace <path> Source MetaSql workspace to compare with the live database. <path> False
meta-sql execute Execute a SQL Server file or query for demo, bootstrap, and verification scripts. 6 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-sql execute --connection-env <value> [--file <path>] [--query <value>] [--quiet] [--timeout-seconds <value>] [--var <value>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--connection-env <value> Environment variable containing the SQL Server connection string. <value> False
--file <path> Execute SQL from a file. Mutually exclusive with --query. <path> False
--query <value> Execute inline SQL. Mutually exclusive with --file. <value> False
--quiet Suppress result-set and success output. False
--timeout-seconds <value> Command timeout for each SQL batch. 0 or omitted means no timeout. <value> False
--var <value> Replace one SQLCMD-style $(NAME) token before execution. <value> False
meta-sql extract sqlserver Extract SQL Server database objects into a MetaSql workspace. 9 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-sql extract sqlserver [--allow-empty] --connection-env <value> [--include-functions] [--include-stored-procedures] [--include-tables] [--include-views] --new-workspace <path> [--schema <value>] [--table <value>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--allow-empty Create an empty database/schema workspace when no tables or modules match. False
--connection-env <value> Environment variable containing the SQL Server connection string. <value> False
--include-functions Extract function modules. If any include switch is provided, only selected object kinds are extracted. False
--include-stored-procedures Extract stored procedure modules. If any include switch is provided, only selected object kinds are extracted. False
--include-tables Extract tables, columns, keys, and indexes. If no include switch is provided, all object kinds are extracted. False
--include-views Extract view modules. If any include switch is provided, only selected object kinds are extracted. False
--new-workspace <path> Directory where the MetaSql workspace will be created. <path> False
--schema <value> Extract only one SQL Server schema. <value> False
--table <value> Extract only one table. SQL module extraction is skipped when a table filter is used. <value> False
meta-sql help Show help. 0 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-sql help
Meta-BI / CLI

meta-tabular deploy Create modeled tabular database objects, including calculation groups, and process unless --no-process is used; processing failures fail the command.
meta-tabular drop Drop a tabular database from an Analysis Services tabular instance with no confirmation prompt.
meta-tabular help Show help.
meta-tabular new-workspace Create a MetaTabular workspace.
meta-tabular process Process an existing tabular database, table, or partition.
meta-tabular restore Promote a processed tabular database through backup and restore for pre-prod-to-prod promotion; restore does not process.

meta-tabular deploy Create modeled tabular database objects, including calculation groups, and process unless --no-process is used; processing failures fail the command. 5 options / 0 examples

Usage

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--database-name <value> Target database name. Defaults to the modeled database name. <value> False
--drop-existing Drop an existing target database before create/deploy. False
--no-process Deploy metadata only and skip full processing. False
--server <value> Analysis Services tabular server. <value> False
--workspace <path> Workspace path. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False

meta-tabular process Process an existing tabular database, table, or partition. 5 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-tabular process --database-name <value> [--partition <value>] [--refresh-type <value>] --server <value> [--table <value>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--database-name <value> Database name to process. <value> False
--partition <value> Partition name or id to process. Requires --table. <value> False
--refresh-type <value> Refresh type. Defaults to Full. Common values: Full, DataOnly, Calculate, ClearValues, Automatic, Add, Defragment. <value> False
--server <value> Analysis Services tabular server. <value> False
--table <value> Table name or id to process instead of the whole database. <value> False
meta-tabular restore Promote a processed tabular database through backup and restore for pre-prod-to-prod promotion; restore does not process. 7 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-tabular restore --backup-file <path> [--drop-existing] [--overwrite-backup-file] --source-database-name <value> --source-server <value> --target-database-name <value> --target-server <value>

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--backup-file <path> Backup file path accessible to the Analysis Services service accounts. <path> False
--drop-existing Drop an existing target database before restore. False
--overwrite-backup-file Overwrite an existing backup file. False
--source-database-name <value> Source processed database name. <value> False
--source-server <value> Source Analysis Services server containing the processed database. <value> False
--target-database-name <value> Target database name to restore. <value> False
--target-server <value> Target Analysis Services server. <value> False
Meta-BI / CLI

meta-transform-binding

Commands

2 commands exposed by meta-transform-binding.

CommandSummary
meta-transform-binding bind Bind transform scripts and validate them against source and target schema contracts into a new workspace.
meta-transform-binding help Show help.
meta-transform-binding bind Bind transform scripts and validate them against source and target schema contracts into a new workspace. 11 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-transform-binding bind [--allow-partial] [--data-type-conversion-workspace <path>] --execute-system <value> [--execute-system-default-schema-name <value>] [--ignore-target-columns <col[,col...]>] [--ignore-target-columns-if-present <col[,col...]>] --new-workspace <path> [--partial-report <path>] --source-schema <path> --target-schema <path> --transform-workspace <path>

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--allow-partial Save only objects that bind and validate successfully. False
--data-type-conversion-workspace <path> Optional sanctioned conversion policy workspace. Omitted uses built-in defaults. <path> False
--execute-system <value> Execution context for one/two-part source identifiers. <value> False
--execute-system-default-schema-name <value> Required when any one-part source identifier exists. <value> True
--ignore-target-columns <col[,col...]> Comma-separated target columns to exclude from target conformance checks. <col[,col...]> False
--ignore-target-columns-if-present <col[,col...]> Comma-separated target columns to exclude only on target tables where they exist. <col[,col...]> False
--new-workspace <path> Directory where the binding workspace will be created. <path> False
--partial-report <path> TSV report for objects skipped due to binding or validation failure. Requires --allow-partial. <path> False
--source-schema <path> Repeatable source MetaSchema workspace. <path> False
--target-schema <path> Target MetaSchema workspace. <path> False
--transform-workspace <path> MetaTransformScript workspace to bind. <path> False
meta-transform-binding help Show help. 0 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-transform-binding help
Meta-BI / CLI

meta-transform-script

Commands

10 commands exposed by meta-transform-script.

CommandSummary
meta-transform-script from sql-code Import SQL text.
meta-transform-script from sql-file Import one .sql file.
meta-transform-script from sql-files Import many .sql files from an explicit manifest.
meta-transform-script help Show help.
meta-transform-script stored-procedure add-contract Add or replace stored procedure contract metadata.
meta-transform-script stored-procedure remove-contract Remove a stored procedure contract and its declared rows.
meta-transform-script stored-procedure view-contract View stored procedure contracts.
meta-transform-script target-identifiers from-pattern Derive view target identifiers from transform script names.
meta-transform-script to sql-code Emit one transform script as SQL text.
meta-transform-script to sql-path Emit SQL scripts to a file or folder.
meta-transform-script from sql-code Import SQL text. 5 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-transform-script from sql-code --code <sql> [--name <value>] [--new-workspace <path>] [--target <sql-identifier>] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--code <sql> SQL text to import. <sql> False
--name <value> Required when SQL code is bare SELECT or mutation text without a CREATE wrapper. <value> True
--new-workspace <path> Create a new MetaTransformScript workspace. <path> False
--target <sql-identifier> Optional for CREATE VIEW imports; required for bare SELECT; not allowed for inline CREATE FUNCTION or mutation imports. <sql-identifier> False
--workspace <path> Add one script to an existing workspace. <path> False
meta-transform-script from sql-file Import one .sql file. 4 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-transform-script from sql-file [--new-workspace <path>] --path <file.sql> [--target <sql-identifier>] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--new-workspace <path> Create a new MetaTransformScript workspace. <path> False
--path <file.sql> SQL file to import. <file.sql> False
--target <sql-identifier> Optional for CREATE VIEW imports; required for bare SELECT; not allowed for inline CREATE FUNCTION or mutation imports. <sql-identifier> False
--workspace <path> Add one script to an existing workspace. <path> False
meta-transform-script from sql-files Import many .sql files from an explicit manifest. 5 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-transform-script from sql-files --manifest <manifest.tsv> [--new-workspace <path>] [--report <path>] [--verbose] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--manifest <manifest.tsv> TSV manifest with a Path column and optional Target column. <manifest.tsv> False
--new-workspace <path> Create a new MetaTransformScript workspace. <path> False
--report <path> Write per-file Success/Failure rows. <path> False
--verbose Print one progress line per imported file. False
--workspace <path> Add successful imports to an existing workspace. <path> False
meta-transform-script help Show help. 0 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-transform-script help
meta-transform-script stored-procedure add-contract Add or replace stored procedure contract metadata. 6 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-transform-script stored-procedure add-contract --name <value> [--notes <value>] [--operation <operation>] [--result-column <rowset>=<column>] [--result-rowset <value>] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--name <value> Stored procedure transform script name. <value> False
--notes <value> Human note stored on the contract. <value> False
--operation <operation> Declare an ordered operation: <ordinal>:<kind>:<sql-id>[=<role>]. <operation> False
--result-column <rowset>=<column> Declare a result column for a named result rowset. <rowset>=<column> False
--result-rowset <value> Declare the optional result rowset. <value> False
--workspace <path> MetaTransformScript workspace to update. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
meta-transform-script stored-procedure remove-contract Remove a stored procedure contract and its declared rows. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-transform-script stored-procedure remove-contract --name <value> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--name <value> Stored procedure transform script name. <value> False
--workspace <path> MetaTransformScript workspace to update. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
meta-transform-script stored-procedure view-contract View stored procedure contracts. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-transform-script stored-procedure view-contract [--name <value>] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--name <value> Inspect one stored procedure transform script by name. <value> False
--workspace <path> MetaTransformScript workspace to inspect. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
meta-transform-script target-identifiers from-pattern Derive view target identifiers from transform script names. 7 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-transform-script target-identifiers from-pattern [--allow-empty] [--dry-run] [--only-missing] --source-pattern <pattern> --target-pattern <pattern> [--verbose] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--allow-empty Exit successfully even when the pattern updates no target identifiers. False
--dry-run Show what would change without saving the workspace. False
--only-missing Skip view scripts that already have a target identifier. False
--source-pattern <pattern> Pattern matched against TransformScript.Name. Tokens use {name}. <pattern> False
--target-pattern <pattern> Pattern rendered into TargetSqlIdentifier from captured source tokens. <pattern> False
--verbose Print each target identifier update. False
--workspace <path> MetaTransformScript workspace to update. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
meta-transform-script to sql-code Emit one transform script as SQL text. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-transform-script to sql-code [--name <value>] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--name <value> Required when the workspace contains multiple scripts. <value> True
--workspace <path> MetaTransformScript workspace to export. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
meta-transform-script to sql-path Emit SQL scripts to a file or folder. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-transform-script to sql-path --out <path> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--out <path> Output .sql file or target folder. <path> False
--workspace <path> MetaTransformScript workspace to export. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
